name: Malik ibn Abd
title: Heavenly Friend
role: Brave Companion and Ally of Sayf
shortDescription: The cousin and maternal brother of Sayf who fought shoulder to shoulder with him with a heart full of love for Imam Hussain (AS) and was martyred.
fullStory:
Malik ibn Abd ibn Sari' was a brave youth and sincere Shia who, along with his cousin and maternal brother 'Sayf ibn al-Harith', were eternal allies. He too, alongside Sayf, reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan to sacrifice his life in the path of defending the truth.

On the day of Ashura, Malik, with a heart full of sorrow for Imam Hussain's (AS) loneliness, came to the Imam with Sayf. His crying was not out of fear of death, but rather because he burned with the thought that he could do no more than sacrifice a single life for his Imam.

After Imam Hussain's (AS) blessing for these two youths, Malik entered the battlefield with Sayf. Their fighting style was such that they covered each other and, like two powerful arms, penetrated the heart of the enemy army.

With his sword, Malik struck down anyone who approached Sayf, and Sayf watched over Malik. This unity and brotherhood on the battlefield terrified the enemies. Malik recited war poetry in the field, reminding them of his family's bravery.

Finally, the multitude of the Kufan army and continuous showering of arrows brought these two warriors down. Malik ibn Abd, his body full of wounds, fell to the ground next to his cousin Sayf, and his pure soul flew to the heavens.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى مالِكِ بْنِ عَبْدِ بْنِ سَريعٍ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Malik ibn Abd and his ally Sayf ibn al-Harith.
